commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Human maintainer handling is needed because the PR is draft, protected by the maintainer label, stacked on a dirty open base, and missing contributor proof that automation cannot supply.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain concern was found; the diff changes Doctor diagnostics, tests, and docs without new dependencies, workflows, or secret-handling expansion. (sha=98f1f25ca5ccb96be6b3dc590491c214702e4039)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a structured `core/doctor/session-locks` health finding and dry-run repair effect for stale session lock files while keeping real cleanup in the legacy Doctor session-lock contribution.

PR surface: Source -12, Tests +637, Docs +2. Total +627 across 14 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a feature PR rather than a bug report. Source inspection shows current main treats session-lock cleanup as legacy Doctor behavior, while the PR adds the structured check without real CLI proof.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human maintainer handling is needed because the PR is draft, protected by the maintainer label, stacked on a dirty open base, and missing contributor proof that automation cannot supply.

target.title: Doctor: expose session lock findings
target.body:
## Summary

- Exposes stale session lock files as structured doctor health findings.
- Adds dry-run repair effects for stale lock cleanup.
- Keeps the existing `doctor:session-locks` legacy `run` as the only real mutating cleanup path for normal `doctor` / `doctor --fix`.
- Wires `core/doctor/session-locks` into the ordered contribution list so lint order stays aligned with doctor order.

This is intentionally the small session-lock slice from the older doctor repair stack. It does not migrate session transcripts, snapshots, state providers, heartbeat repair, or state-integrity repair.

## Stack

Stacked on #86627. The only new session-lock commit in this PR is `55315026e61`; the session-lock delta is 6 files.

## Current Head

Rebased and signed head: `55315026e61`.

- 2026-06-03 replayed onto refreshed #86627 head `9bb31f37831c`; signed head `98f1f25ca5c`.

## Validation

- `git diff --check`
- `node node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/commands/doctor-session-locks.ts src/commands/doctor-session-locks.test.ts src/commands/doctor-lint.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts`
- `node node_modules/oxlint/bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/commands/doctor-session-locks.ts src/commands/doctor-session-locks.test.ts src/commands/doctor-lint.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental false --pretty false`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit --incremental false --pretty false`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-session-locks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/commands/doctor-lint.test.ts --reporter=dot --pool=forks --testTimeout=30000 --hookTimeout=30000` (4 files, 62 tests)
- 2026-06-03 refresh on signed head `55315026e61`: `git diff --check`; targeted `oxlint`; `pnpm exec vitest run --project unit-fast src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts`; `pnpm exec vitest run --project commands src/commands/doctor-state-integrity.test.ts src/commands/doctor-lint.test.ts`; `pnpm exec vitest run --project unit src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts`.
- 2026-06-03 replayed onto fixed #86627 base; signed head `55315026e61`.
